logo.alt: OpenFlashcards - Self-hosted flashcard app with spaced repetition
logo.title: OpenFlashcards

section1.title: Learn more in less time with flashcards
section1.paragraph: An app for flashcards with spaced repetition and smart features to help you master any subject at your own pace.

section2.title: Transform any content into active learning

card1.title: You choose what you want to learn
card1.description: Create your own decks with question and answer cards. From languages to exact sciences, everything works.

card2.title: Study with spaced repetition
card2.description: The app identifies your difficulty and organizes cards for you to review at the ideal time.

card3.title: Your study day ready for you
card3.description: Every day, your priority decks will be on the home page, with suggestions of what to study.

card4.title: Track your daily progress
card4.description: Study consistently and track your performance. Visualize your study journey with clarity and purpose.

textToSpeech.title: Text to Speech
textToSpeech.description: Listen to the correct pronunciation of your card texts through the text-to-speech feature. Perfect for language training and absorbing content more naturally.

decks.title: Create as many decks as you want
decks.description: Add as many decks and cards as you want, at your own pace and in your own way. Organize by topics, subjects, or languages, and add as many cards as needed for your study.

ai.title: AI that accelerates your learning
ai.description: The app will feature AI capabilities that will transform your way of studying, analyzing your performance and adapting content intelligently.

footer.copyright: OpenFlashcards. Open source under the MIT license.

whatIs.question: What is OpenFlashcards?
whatIs.answer: OpenFlashcards is a flashcard app that uses spaced repetition powered by the FSRS algorithm to help you learn and retain information more effectively. It's designed for students, professionals, and lifelong learners.

spacedRepetition.question: What is spaced repetition?
spacedRepetition.answer: Spaced repetition is a learning technique that schedules reviews at optimal intervals based on how well you know each card. Cards you find difficult appear more frequently, while those you know well are shown less often, maximizing learning efficiency.

fsrs.question: What is the FSRS algorithm?
fsrs.answer: FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) is a modern, research-backed algorithm that adapts to your personal learning patterns. It's more accurate than traditional algorithms like SM-2, helping you study smarter, not harder.

free.question: Is OpenFlashcards free?
free.answer: Yes. OpenFlashcards is free and open source under the MIT license. Self-host it yourself, or check the README for hosting options.

languages.question: Can I use it to learn languages?
languages.answer: Absolutely! OpenFlashcards is perfect for language learning. With features like text-to-speech for pronunciation practice and unlimited decks, you can create vocabulary, grammar, and phrase cards for any language.
